q: The burning wood in the air releases _______ and light energies.
The firework displays illustrates a spectacular release of _______ (light and heat) in a chemical reaction.
_______ is the chemical reaction that occurs when plants use energy from the sun, carbon dioxide and water to synthesize the food that the plants need to survive.
When we burn fossil fuels for energy, _______ changes take place during the reaction.
Except for the energy produced from _______, all of man's energy resources are chemicals or involve the utilisation of energy through chemical reactions.
Whenever a chemical reaction (chemical change) occurs, _______ is usually transferred to or from the surroundings.
Changing states of _______ (physical change) also involve energy changes.
_______ energy is formed due to the movement.
_______ energy is formed due to due to the position.
Energy can neither be created nor _______.
Energy can change from one form to another without a net loss or gain of energy and it is called _______.
Electric motor can transform electrical energy into _______ energy.
Hair dryer can change from _______ energy into thermal energy.
Battery can transform _______ energy into electrical energy.
Automobile engine can transform _______ energy into mechanical energy.
Solar cell can transform _______ energy into electrical energy.
All chemical reactions involve energy _______.
Whether a chemical reaction absorbs or releases energy, the _______ is not changed during the reaction.
_______ energy is the energy that is stored in the bonds of chemical compounds such as atoms and molecules.
_______ changes take place during chemical reactions.
In some reactions, the energy changes can be observed as either an _______ or a decrease in the overall energy of the system.
Chemical energy is the _______ of most of the energy that humans need to function.
Humans release this chemical energy during _______ and use it to power systems inside the body.
Plants store chemical energy in _______ formed during photosynthesis.
Whenever a chemical reaction takes place between reactants to form products, there is always an energy change usually in the form of _______.
Chemical energy often involves changes in energy due to the breaking and formation of _______.
The _______ change is due to the difference in the amounts of stored chemical energy between the reactant and the product particles in a chemical reaction.
Chemical reactions provide _______ which is the result of interactions between ions in an electrolyte.
A process that can take place on its own accord is called _______.
In a spontaneous reaction, the physical or chemical change occurs without the addition of _______.
Adding a solution of dilute sulphuric acid to a solution of sodium hydroxide is a(an) _______ reaction.
Most of the reaction will not take place until the system has some amount of _______.
Iron rusting is _______ and spontaneous.
When the ice melts at room temperature, that is a spontaneous and _______ process.
When sulphuric acid is slowly poured into water, the flask or beaker becomes _______.
_______ reactions give out energy. So, there is a temperature rise.
In exothermic reaction, the energy of the product is _______ than the energy of the reactants.
When ammonium chloride is dissolved in water, the flask becomes quite _______ to the touch.
Endothermic reaction is heat being absorbed from the _______ by the reacting system.
Melting of ice, cooking of egg, evaporating liquid water, thermal decomposition of limestone are examples of _______ reactions.
Endothermic reactions take in energy from the surroundings. So, there is _______ fall.
In an endothermic reaction, the energy of the product is _______ than the energy of the reactants.
Energy changes in chemical reactions are caused by the making and breaking of chemical _______.
Energy is absorbed to break bonds and bond breaking is an _______ process.
Energy is _______ when new bonds form and bond making is an exothermic process.
The total chemical energy of a substance is called its _______.
The change in enthalpy describes the amount of heat transferred at constant _______ during a chemical reaction.
Heat changes can be measured by using a _______.
The heat absorbed or released in a process occurring at constant _______ is called the enthalpy change.
Symbol of enthalpy change is _______.
For an endothermic reaction, energy is _______ from the surroundings.
For an _______ reaction, the total energy of the products is greater than that of reactants.
For an _______ reaction, the total energy of the products is lower than that of reactants.
The enthalpy change for an exothermic reaction is assigned by _______ sign.
The enthalpy change for an endothermic reaction is assigned by _______ sign.
The standard conditions chosen for energy changes are 25°C and _______ pressure.
A _______ equation is a balanced equation that includes the physical states of all reactants and products and the standard enthalpy change.
The standard enthalpy change of formation of a compound is the enthalpy change when one mole of a compound is formed from its elements under _______ conditions.
The standard enthalpy change of _______ is the enthalpy change when one mole of a substance (element or compound) is burnt completely in oxygen under standard conditions.
The standard enthalpy change of _______ is the enthalpy change when solutions of an acid and an alkali react together under standard conditions to produce one mole of water.
The standard enthalpy change of neutralisation is the enthalpy change when solutions of an acid and an alkali react together under standard conditions to produce _______ of water.
A _______ is a device to measure the amount of heat released or absorbed in reactions that occur in solutions.
The heat change can be measured through the _______.
A process in which the system absorbs heat from the surroundings is called _______.
The symbol of the standard enthalpy change is _______.
Heat liberated by a chemical reaction is assigned by _______.
Change in enthalpy of a system is due to the heat supplied at a constant _______.
The highly accurate values of enthalpy change of combustion can be measured by using a special apparatus called _______.
The enthalpy change of solution, ΔH_sol, of a substance, is the enthalpy change when one mole of the substance is dissolved in _______.
The amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of _______ of water by one degree is known as the specific heat capacity of water.
A _______ is a type of calorimeter for determining the heat of combustion.
The highly accurate values of the standard enthalpy change of _______ can be measured only by a bomb calorimeter.
The enthalpy change for a chemical reaction is the same, whatever _______ is taken from reactants to products.
Enthalpy changes are due to the breaking and forming of _______.
Breaking a bond _______ energy
Making a bond _______ energy.
_______ is the amount of energy required in bond breaking or making of one mole of chemical bonds in a molecular element or compound.
The higher the _______, the more energy needed to break the bond and the stronger is the bond.
The neutralisation reaction is exothermic and the enthalpy change is _______.
Law of conservation of energy states that in any physical or physical process, energy can neither be _______ nor destroyed.
Energy _______ is the process of changing one form of energy into another.
_______ is the specific part of the universe that contains the reaction or process which is studied.
Surroundings are everything in the _______ other than the system.
_______ reaction is a reaction which releases heat into the surroundings.
_______ reaction is a reaction which absorbs heat from the surroundings.
_______ is the change accompanying a chemical reaction at constant pressure.
Specific heat capacity of liquid is the energy to _______ the temperature of 1 g of liquid by 1°C.
